title: Ancient India
description: Practice your skills with a quiz on the Indus River Valley.

assessment_items.question_content:
Refer to the image below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

**The bath pictured above best represents what _similarity_ between ancient civilizations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.caption: Great bath at Mohenjo-Daro. Image credit: [Wikimedia]( https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Bath,_Mohenjo-daro#/media/File:Mohenjodaro_Sindh.jpeg).

choices.content: Urban planning
Creation of legal codes
Codification of religious beliefs
Interregional trade

assessment_items.question_content:
Refer to the images below. 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

**Taken together, the two images best support which of the following similarities between early civilizations?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.caption: Image 1: Standardized weights found at Harappa. Image credit: [Harappa Archaeological Research Project](https://www.harappa.com/slide/weights-harappa)
Image 2: Ziggurat at Ur in Mesopotamia. Image credit: [Wikimedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ziggurat_of_Ur#/media/File:Ancient_ziggurat_at_Ali_Air_Base_Iraq_2005.jpg)

choices.content: Centralized authority controlled production and distribution of resources
Trade relations with neighboring civilizations increased
Social hierarchies intensified in response to trade
Systems of record keeping arose to facilitate monumental building

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question.

>"Thy Steers were steady, kept in place by holy verse and Sama-hymn: All car were thy two chariot wheels: thy path was tremulous in the sky. Clean, as thou wentest, were thy wheels wind, was the axle fastened there. Surya, proceeding to her Lord, mounted a spirit-fashioned car."

Excerpt from _Rig Veda_, Book 10, Hymn 85 c. 1500 BCE

**A historian would most likely use this passage from the Rig Veda to argue for which of the following developments in the Indus Valley?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The invention of chariots
The continued use of war chariots
The introduction of chariots by Indo-Aryans
The use of chariots to support agriculture

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question.

>"Hail Aśvins [gods of healing], rich in treasure, Lords of splendor, having nimble hands, accept the sacrificial food. Hail Aśvins, rich in wondrous deeds, Hail heroes worthy of our praise, accept our songs with mighty thought."

Excerpt from _Rigveda_, Book 1, Hymn 3

**The composition of the verses above was most representative of which historical development?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Codified religious beliefs
Centralized states
Legal codes
Monumental architecture

assessment_items.question_content:
Refer to the image below.

[[☃ image 1]]

**Seals such as the one pictured above could best be used as early evidence for what historical continuity?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: Indus Harappan seal, similar to a seal found in ancient Mesopotamia
options.caption: Indus Harappan seal, similar to a seal found in ancient Mesopotamia. Image credit: [Harappa.com](https://www.harappa.com/blog/one-or-two-unicorn-horns)

choices.content: The existence of trade between regions
The spread of religious belief systems
The use of art to reinforce political power
The codification of legal systems for public display

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question. 

_Note that “Dasyu” and “Dasa” were terms that meant roughly “enemy” or “demon” in the Vedic texts, and that rites were central to the Vedic religion._

>"Around us is the Dasyu, riteless, void of sense, inhuman, keeping alien laws. Baffle, you Slayer of the foe, the weapon which this Dasa wields."

Excerpt from _Rigveda_, Book 10, Hymn 12

**The ideas expressed in this passage are best understood in the context of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Indo-European migration into northern India
The growth of monotheism in Vedic society
Religious challenges to social hierarchy
Codification of laws based on Vedic texts

assessment_items.question_content:
Read the passage below and answer the question.

>"When they divided Purusha, how many portions did they make? What did they call his mouth, his arms? What do they call his thighs and feet? The Brahman was his mouth, of both his arms was the Rajanya made. His thighs became the Vaisya. From his feet the Sudra was produced."

Excerpt from _Rigveda_, Book 10, Hymn 90

**The __ideas__ expressed in this passage most directly contributed to which of the following developments in ancient Indian society?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Social hierarchy based on castes
Human sacrifice as religious ritual
Population growth due to increased food supply
Political divisions among Indian elites
